prompt: I've been working on two things: 1) Applying code review feedback from PR #347 — I finished items 1-3 (error wrapping, cache key constant, context parameter) but still have items 4-5 (cache metrics and product validation). 2) Started implementing cache invalidation — I created pkg/cache/invalidation.go with a basic Invalidate method but still need to add pattern-based invalidation and wire it into UpdateProduct. I discovered the cache uses a write-through pattern. The code review feedback is tracked in docs/code-review-feedback.md. Create a handoff.
assertions: A handoff markdown file was created in a thoughts/handoffs/ or thoughts/shared/handoffs/ directory
The handoff file has valid YAML frontmatter containing at least date and branch fields
The handoff document is under 200 lines total
The Tasks section clearly separates the two concerns: code review feedback and cache invalidation feature
The Tasks section tracks which code review items are done (1-3) and which remain (4-5)
The Learnings section mentions the write-through cache pattern discovery
Action items include both remaining code review items (4-5) and cache invalidation wiring
The document does not contain code blocks longer than 10 lines
The response to the user includes the /resume-handoff command with the handoff file path
